content: Ruth said to Naomi: "Your God shall be ___"
explanation: Ruth 1:16 - Ruth chose to cling to Naomi and to her God. Ruth's faithfulness is a model of faith.

content: Ruth was the great-grandmother of which famous king in the Bible?
explanation: Ruth 4:17 - Ruth gave birth to Obed, Obed fathered Jesse, and Jesse fathered David. Ruth is also in the genealogy of Jesus (Matthew 1:5).

content: Why did Naomi's family leave Bethlehem for the land of Moab?
explanation: Ruth 1:1 - A famine in Bethlehem (which means "house of bread") drove Elimelech's family to the land of Moab.

content: What were the names of Naomi's two Moabite daughters-in-law?
explanation: Ruth 1:4 - Naomi's two sons married Ruth and Orpah in Moab.

content: How did Orpah and Ruth respond differently when Naomi told them to go back?
explanation: Ruth 1:14-16 - Orpah chose safety; Ruth chose faithfulness. Two choices led to two very different lives.
options: Orpah returned to Moab; Ruth stayed and followed Naomi

content: Naomi changed her name to Mara. What does Mara mean?
explanation: Ruth 1:20 - Naomi (meaning "pleasant") changed her name to Mara (meaning "bitter") after losing her husband and two sons.

content: What did Ruth do to provide for Naomi?
explanation: Ruth 2:2 - Ruth gleaned grain under the law that allowed the poor to gather leftover grain in the fields (Leviticus 19:9).
options: Gleaned (gathered leftover grain) in the fields

content: What did Boaz commend Ruth for?
explanation: Ruth 2:11-12 - Boaz recognized Ruth's faith and loyalty. The Lord cares for those who come to take refuge under His wings.
options: Leaving her homeland to follow her mother-in-law to a foreign land, taking refuge under the Lord's wings

content: What did Ruth ask Boaz to do at the threshing floor?
explanation: Ruth 3:9 - "Spread the corner of your garment over your servant, for you are a kinsman-redeemer (go'el)." This is a picture of the Lord redeeming us.

content: The concept of the "go'el" (kinsman-redeemer) in the book of Ruth is a picture of whom?
explanation: Boaz acts as go'el: he redeems the land, marries Ruth, and preserves the family line. Jesus acts as go'el: He redeems us from sin, marries the Church, and gives us life.

content: Why did the closer relative refuse the right of redemption?
explanation: Ruth 4:6 - The closer kinsman-redeemer refused because he feared it would jeopardize his own inheritance. Boaz was willing to sacrifice.
options: He feared it would endanger his own inheritance

content: Boaz married Ruth and redeemed whose estate?
explanation: Ruth 4:9-10 - Boaz redeemed the entire estate and married Ruth to preserve the name of the deceased.

content: What was the name of Boaz and Ruth's son?
explanation: Ruth 4:17 - Obed was the father of Jesse, the grandfather of David. Ruth was the great-grandmother of the royal line and of Jesus.
